opp.ais_why:
• The second presentation in June 2025 included AI-driven contract discovery and risk-scoring demo plus automatic negotiation reference. Customer Carlos Dolz acknowledged the AI features but pushed back on accuracy and budget ‚Äî buyer-side AI scepticism rather than AI demand. Product scope (iContract, iRisk, iSupplier) has no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze. The scope itself doesn't support an AI-led deal even if Carlos's scepticism reverses.
• Qualified stage for ~12 months with limited progression. 3 Avoma meetings all in 2025 (latest June 2025) ‚Äî significant dormancy. Carlos objected to 5-year commitment, preferring annual. Small procurement team (3 users). BD_Engagement Unengaged. The combination of long dormancy + buyer AI scepticism + small team + no AI products in scope is multiply consistent with AI Resistant.
• Medium confidence: AI features were demo'd but customer focus is conservative SRM-only scope, hosting/security, and modular pricing. Stagnation and no AI products in scope override the AI demo signal. Score 3 reflects buyer-articulated AI-accuracy concerns plus traditional product scope. Would only move UP with new engagement and scope expansion.

sources: Avoma call 5 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
Avoma call 6 May 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista (EUR 20M Spanish spend)
Avoma call 25 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated strong qualitative pain but never quantified the value case. Carlos Dolz Bautista (Sourcing Associate) said on 5 Jun 2025: 'we spend a lot of time trying to control, and also we miss information because at the end you can lose email... everything is manual.' He manages ~600 suppliers across many business units (ambulances, doctors, hospitals, psychologists, transport) on Excel, controlling ~EUR 20M of Spanish annual spend (stated 6 May 2025). On 25 Jun 2025 he added 'a lot of invoices that we need to control manually... the mistakes can be higher because you don't control the people entering the Excel.' No cost-of-error, FTE burden, or projected savings has ever been put to a number, which is exactly why the internal business case has stalled. Quantifying the manual-process cost is a required move.

sources: Avoma calls 6 May / 5 Jun / 25 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
champion.narrative: Carlos Dolz Bautista (Sourcing Associate) is a developing champion, not a power champion. He drove all three 2025 calls, articulated the pain and requirements in detail, praised the platform ('your solution is quite robust... it's a good solution', 25 Jun 2025), and personally re-opened the deal after the freeze ('we would like to revisit this conversation in mid-2026', 29 Apr 2026). But he has no budget authority, has never introduced a decision-maker, legal, IT, or finance despite Zycus's requests, and deflected on widening the room ('by the moment it's only us'). What has developed: sustained engagement and genuine advocacy for solving the problem. What is missing: internal selling power and access to whoever funds the ~EUR 20M budget. He is at risk of going quiet, having not replied to the 15 Jun 2026 re-contact.

sources: Avoma call 6 May 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
Avoma call 5 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
Avoma call 25 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
competition.narrative: The real competitor is the do-nothing / stay-on-Excel status quo, sustained by budget uncertainty and organizational inertia rather than a rival vendor. Oracle ERP is the incumbent for finance and POs and stays in place; Carlos called it 'a closed system... we see limitations in the flexibility we need' (6 May 2025), which is the wedge for Zycus as the flexible upstream procurement layer that integrates with Oracle downstream. Coupa was evaluated ~2020-2021 during the Oracle selection and was not chosen ('we were analyzing Coupa and all different models'), and has not resurfaced. Zycus met every functional requirement in the demos, so we win on fit; we lose to inertia unless we quantify ROI and reach a funder.

sources: Avoma call 5 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
Avoma call 25 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is well established and owner-attributed. Carlos (Sourcing Associate, the pain owner) described a fully manual, Excel-based process for ~600 suppliers: 'everything is handmade, it's very manual... we spend a lot of time trying to control, and we miss information because you can lose email' (5 Jun 2025). Certificate/document renewals across regulated medical suppliers are tracked by hand and risk lapsing, and invoices are 'controlled manually' wasting team time (25 Jun 2025). The pain is real and recurring, but it is felt at the operational level by a 3-person team, not yet framed as a financial or compliance risk that a budget owner would fund.

sources: Avoma call 25 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista and Pierre Meraud
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are essentially unknown. The only concrete signals are that Teladoc uses DocuSign ('we are working with DocuSign', 25 Jun 2025) and that Zycus requires a minimum 4-to-5-year term, which Carlos resisted in favor of annual renewals. Pierre floated a trial-then-commit structure with an exit clause ('a trial period... then a 4 or 5 years contract... I can negotiate internally an exit clause', 25 Jun 2025) but nothing was formalized. Legal, procurement, InfoSec, and MSA/SOW steps have never been mapped. Visibility would come from securing a written phased-commercial proposal and asking Carlos who owns contract sign-off and vendor security review.

sources: Avoma call 5 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
Avoma call 6 May 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
Avoma call 25 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer has been identified after 13 months. Carlos confirmed on 5 Jun 2025 'it's only between Natasha, Vincent and me... 3 people' and on 6 May 2025 that New York HQ 'don't know' the detail and would only be looped in for reporting: 'the key decisions... will be in our side'. So budget authority sits somewhere above this 3-person sourcing team but has never been named or engaged, and the cost question Carlos flagged ('how long to develop and the cost are the two important questions', 25 Jun 2025) has no owner. Naming and reaching whoever signs the ~EUR 20M procurement budget in the Barcelona international unit is the single highest-value MEDDPICC move.

sources: Avoma call 6 May 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running an informal, self-directed benchmark, not a structured RFP. Carlos described it on 6 May 2025 as 'just starting to check the market benchmarking... it's more like by ourselves', with the international Barcelona unit deciding independently and HQ merely informed. The intended path (shortlist July 2025, decision September 2025, implementation early 2026) collapsed into an 8-month freeze and a stated 'revisit mid-2026'. The approval chain above the 3-person team is undefined. There is no mutual close plan, no confirmed procurement/legal gate, and the buyer's cadence is now driven entirely by their internal analysis rather than any external event.

sources: Avoma call 25 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
Avoma call 5 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are clear from the discovery and demo calls: supplier onboarding and registration with enforced document/certificate control, a contract repository with expiry alerts and clause-completeness checks, flexible roles/permissions across heterogeneous business units, integration with Oracle for downstream PO/invoice, and above all data hosting/compliance for sensitive medical data. Carlos said on 25 Jun 2025 the hosting question 'is critical to know where you save it... we have medical information... it's a high risk for us', and Pierre confirmed EU hosting in a Frankfurt Azure data center. Commercial flexibility is also a stated criterion: Carlos wants a trial before any multi-year commitment ('maybe we bought a Ferrari and actually we need a shot'). Sourcing and PO automation are explicitly out of initial scope.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — The single strongest current threat is the do-nothing / stay-on-Excel status quo, driven by budget uncertainty and a 13-month stall with no funder engaged, not by any active rival. Oracle ERP is the retained incumbent for finance/PO (low threat, and the wedge for Zycus as the flexible upstream layer). Coupa was evaluated and passed over ~2020-2021 and has faded with no re-engagement. Zycus met every functional requirement in the demos, so the fight is against inertia, not a competitor.
bullets.text: A competitor is pushing back — The single strongest current threat is the do-nothing / stay-on-Excel status quo, driven by budget uncertainty and a 13-month stall with no funder engaged, not…

AI demand is absent, not merely under-surfaced.

So budget authority sits somewhere above this 3-person sourcing team but has never been named or engaged, and the cost question Carlos flagged ('how long to develop and the cost are the two important questions', 25 Jun 2025) has no owner. Naming and reaching whoever signs the ~EUR 20M procurement budget in the Barcelona international unit is the single highest-value MEDDPICC move.

Scope (iContract, iSupplier, iRisk) carries no Merlin / agentic AI, and Carlos actively pushed back on AI accuracy in the demo ('this data, where did it come from? it has to be checked before'). The AI features are helpful proof points for time-saving, not the buying trigger.

What has developed is trust and problem alignment; what is missing is internal selling power and access to a funder.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Teladoc Health Inc — Qualified · $250K · closes 22 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); momentum 52 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 3 / Negative is accurate against the calls: AI contract-discovery and autonomous-negotiation features were demoed but the buyer met them with scepticism on accuracy and cost, and the SRM-only scope has no AI products. AI demand is absent, not merely under-surfaced.

items.change: Rep re-contacted Carlos (Clari email) to restart the S2P project; no buyer reply yet.
Carlos replied that no decision has been reached and asked to revisit mid-2026.
Close date pushed from 30 Jun 2026 to 22 Dec 2026.
Second (deep) product demo delivered covering iContract, iSupplier and iRisk.
Project frozen by the buyer pending internal analysis; 'relaunch mid 26'.

deal_movement.summary: Rep-driven, not buyer-driven. The last genuine buyer engagement was Carlos's 29 Apr 2026 email committing to a mid-2026 revisit; the 15 Jun 2026 activity is a rep-side Clari re-contact still awaiting reply. All three substantive buyer calls are from 2025 and the deal has been effectively frozen since Aug 2025.

No buyer-initiated contact since the 29 Apr 2026 'revisit mid-2026' email.
15 Jun 2026 rep re-contact has received no response.
confidence_signals.summary: Buyer confidence has thinned to near-zero: consistent 10% logged confidence, an 8-month freeze, and no reply to the latest rep outreach. Engagement is rep-pushed, not buyer-pulled.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 28 May 2025 (~13 months old), still Qualified. Close date 22 Dec 2026 (~172 days out, already slipped twice: 2025-12-01 -> 2026-02-10 -> 2026-06-30 -> 2026-12-22). Last buyer touch was Carlos's 29 Apr 2026 email ('revisit mid-2026'); the 15 Jun 2026 activity is a rep-side Clari chase awaiting reply. Buyer's own timeline (shortlist Jul 2025, decision Sep 2025) has slipped ~9 months. Three buyer calls, all in 2025 (6 May, 5 Jun, 25 Jun); none in the last 12 months.
evidence: Buyer stated 29 Apr 2026 (Next_Step__c): 'we have not reached a decision yet... we would like to revisit this conversation in mid-2026', with no confirmed milestone.
Carlos Dolz Bautista (Sourcing Associate) said on the 5 Jun 2025 call the decision was expected September 2025 with implementation early 2026; neither happened.
Project was frozen from Aug 2025 through Apr 2026 ('project frozen should be relaunch mid 26', Next_Step__c).
Every Next_Step entry from May 2025 to Jun 2026 logs 10% confidence; forecast category is Pipeline.
AIS is 3 / Negative: the buyer pushed back on AI accuracy and the product scope (iContract, iSupplier, iRisk) carries no Merlin / agentic AI.
Single-threaded on a 3-person sourcing team with no budget authority; New York HQ is informed but not engaged.
north_star_verdict.headline: 13 months in Qualified at 10% confidence, frozen Aug 2025 to Apr 2026; buyer promised a mid-2026 revisit but has not replied to the 15 Jun 2026 re-contact, so the deal is stalled on the buyer's internal analysis with no EB, no budget, and no next milestone.

flags: Multi-thread past Carlos to a budget owner in the Barcelona international unit; 13 months single-threaded on a 3-person sourcing team with no budget authority is the primary blocker.
Convert qualitative pain into a quantified business case (600 suppliers, EUR 20M spend, certificate-lapse and renewal risk) so Carlos has fundable numbers to sell internally.
Set a hard qualify-in/qualify-out checkpoint: after 13 months at 10% confidence, negative AIS, and a missed mid-2026 relaunch, decide relaunch-with-a-date or move to closed-lost.
best_practice_check.summary: Win levers for a stalled, single-threaded early-stage deal: reach a funder, make the pain fundable, and force a qualify decision.

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest current threat is the do-nothing / stay-on-Excel status quo, driven by budget uncertainty and a 13-month stall with no funder engaged, not by any active rival. Oracle ERP is the retained incumbent for finance/PO (low threat, and the wedge for Zycus as the flexible upstream layer). Coupa was evaluated and passed over ~2020-2021 and has faded with no re-engagement. Zycus met every functional requirement in the demos, so the fight is against inertia, not a competitor.

competitors.name: Excel / manual status quo (do-nothing)
competitors.quote: everything is handmade, it's very manual... we spend a lot of time trying to control, and we miss information because you can lose email.
competitors.source: Avoma call 5 Jun 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of the manual process (FTE hours, certificate-lapse compliance risk on 600 regulated suppliers, renewal misses) and pair it with a low-risk trial-then-phased-commit to de-risk the spend.

competitors.name: Oracle ERP (Fusion)
competitors.quote: Oracle in our experience is kind of a closed system... we see limitations in connecting all the flexibility we need from the different business units.
competitors.source: Avoma call 6 May 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus as the flexible upstream procurement layer (supplier onboarding, contracts, sourcing) that feeds Oracle for PO/invoice; Oracle stays for finance, Zycus solves the multi-business-unit complexity it cannot.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: we were analyzing Coupa and all different models... we decided to include Oracle.
competitors.source: Avoma call 6 May 2025, Carlos Dolz Bautista
competitors.how_we_win: Not currently in play; if it resurfaces, lead with Zycus AI-assisted contract intelligence and flexible multi-BU configuration versus Coupa rigidity.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: This is not an AI-led deal and should not be positioned as one. Scope (iContract, iSupplier, iRisk) carries no Merlin / agentic AI, and Carlos actively pushed back on AI accuracy in the demo ('this data, where did it come from? it has to be checked before'). The AI features are helpful proof points for time-saving, not the buying trigger.

items.criterion: Contract repository with expiry alerts and clause-completeness checks

items.quote: you can start with some module... and then in the second phase put in place the sourcing or the procurement part.
items.criterion: Modular / phased scope (start with SRM + contracts, sourcing/PO later)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Functionally Zycus is well aligned to the stated need (supplier onboarding, document control, contract repository with alerts, Oracle integration); the exposed edges are data-hosting assurance for sensitive medical data and the commercial term structure.

Product scope (iContract, iRisk, iSupplier) has no Merlin, no ANA, no Agentic AI, no ML iAnalyze. The scope itself doesn't support an AI-led deal even if Carlos's scepticism reverses.
